summary: Both backend and frontend tested end-to-end for the French restaurant ordering app. All 12 backend pytest tests pass (menu 13 categories, 12 seeded tables, full order lifecycle: open -> add item with options -> update qty -> send -> delete -> close, status filter, 404 errors). Frontend Expo web preview renders correctly on mobile viewport: 12-table grid with free/occupied color states, covers selection modal (chips 1-8), order screen with quantity +/- / Envoyer / Cloturer buttons, menu screen with horizontal category bar (13 cats) + items grid showing option indicators, orders history screen with 'En cours' / 'Clôturées' tabs.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') - consider migrating to FastAPI lifespan handler (non-blocking).
Startup seed deletes the entire menu collection on every reload; OK for demo but would drop runtime menu edits if any were ever added.
seed_data_creation: Tests create an open order on a free table and close it at the end (test_07) so no leftover state. One order was left open during Playwright UI exploration (Table 1, Entrecote 28€).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend testIDs in place: table-card-{n}, covers-input, confirm-open-btn, back-btn, add-btn, order-item-{id}, order-total, send-btn, close-btn, menu-back-btn, view-order-btn, cat-{key}, item-{id}, orders-btn. Note: covers chip values (1-8) are visible buttons but do NOT have individual data-testid - use covers-input + confirm-open-btn for tests. After tapping an occupied table-card-1, app navigates directly to order screen (no modal). Free tables open the covers modal.
